claimText: Solid-State Batteries use solid electrolyte solutions, eliminating the need for a separate separator.
evidenceExcerpt: Solid-State Batteries use solid electrolyte solutions, eliminating the need for a different separator.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing SSB components to conventional batteries.

claimText: Solid-State Batteries offer improved safety because their solid electrolytes are less prone to leakage or combustion compared to liquid electrolytes.
evidenceExcerpt: They offer improved safety as solid electrolytes are less prone to leakage or combustion.
distractorHooks: SSBs are more prone to leakage than liquid batteries

claimText: Solid-State Batteries have the potential to store more energy in the same volume or weight, indicating a higher energy density.
evidenceExcerpt: SSBs also have the potential to store more energy in the same volume or weight
examUse: Statement-based question on the advantages of SSBs.
distractorHooks: SSBs require more volume to store the same amount of energy

claimText: Solid-State Batteries are capable of recharging 4 to 6 times faster than conventional liquid electrolyte batteries.
evidenceExcerpt: can recharge 4 to 6 times faster than liquid electrolyte batteries.
examUse: Statement-based question on charging speeds of next-gen batteries.
distractorHooks: SSBs charge slower than liquid electrolyte batteries
SSBs have the same charging speed as conventional batteries
